title: 10 best herbal capsules for jaundice
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for jaundice are dietary supplements that contain a blend of herbs and natural ingredients, which are believed to support liver function and promote the elimination of bilirubin from the body. Jaundice is a condition characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes, which occurs due to an accumulation of bilirubin in the bloodstream. Examples of herbal ingredients commonly found in jaundice capsules include milk thistle, turmeric, dandelion root, and licorice root. These herbs have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and liver-protective properties, which can help to improve liver function, reduce inflammation, and promote the excretion of bilirubin. By taking herbal capsules for jaundice, individuals can experience improved liver health, reduced jaundice symptoms, and an overall improvement in their quality of life. For instance, a person with jaundice may notice a decrease in skin yellowing, increased energy levels, and a reduction in liver-related symptoms such as abdominal pain and itching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsule helps with jaundice because it promotes the production of bile and its flow from the liver, which is crucial in the treatment of jaundice. Jaundice is a condition characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes due to the buildup of bilirubin in the body. Dandelion contains potent antioxidants that help to protect the liver and promote its function, which aids in the breakdown of bilirubin. Furthermore, dandelion has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the liver, thereby promoting its healing and recovery. Therefore, dandelion capsules can be an effective supplement in managing jaundice and supporting overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Diuretic: Dandelion capsules promote urine production, which helps eliminate bilirubin from the body, reducing jaundice symptoms. This property is due to the presence of taraxacin and taraxacerin.

Hepatoprotective: Dandelion capsules protect the liver from damage, supporting its function in bilirubin metabolism and alleviating jaundice. The active constituents responsible for this effect include sesquiterpene lactones, taraxasterol, and taraxacin.

Cholagogue: Dandelion capsules stimulate bile production and flow, aiding in the elimination of bilirubin from the body and reducing jaundice. The main active compounds responsible for this property are taraxacin and taraxacoside.

Antioxidant: Dandelion capsules contain antioxidants that protect liver cells from oxidative stress and support liver function in jaundice treatment. The antioxidant properties come from vitamins A, C, and D, as well as various flavonoids such as luteolin and quercetin.

Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion capsules reduce inflammation in the liver, promoting healing and reducing jaundice symptoms. This property is attributed to the presence of phenolic acids, triterpenes, and sesquiterpene lactones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain bitter compounds that can help improve bile flow and reduce inflammation, making them useful for treating jaundice.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and can support liver function, helping to reduce the symptoms of jaundice.
Flowers: Contain bioactive compounds like luteolin and apigenin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, aiding in the treatment of jaundice.
Buds: Similar to flowers, they contain beneficial compounds that can help improve liver function and reduce jaundice symptoms.
Seeds: Rich in essential fatty acids and phytosterols, which can support liver health and help in the management of jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 cups of fresh dandelion leaves and flowers, ensuring they are free from pesticides and other contaminants.
Thoroughly clean the dandelion parts, then dry them in a well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator for about 2-3 days until they become crisp.
Grind the dried dandelion leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or herb mill for approximately 2-3 minutes.
Measure out 500-600mg of the ground dandelion powder per capsule, using a capsule filling machine to evenly distribute the powder into size "00" vegetarian capsules.
Seal the capsules and store in airtight containers, keeping them in a cool, dry location away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsule helps with jaundice because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compound. Jaundice is a condition characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes due to the accumulation of bilirubin in the body. Curcumin helps reduce bilirubin levels by stimulating the production of bile and promoting its flow from the liver to the small intestine. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help protect liver cells from damage, promoting liver health and function. Furthermore, its anti-inflammatory effects help reduce inflammation in the liver, thereby alleviating jaundice symptoms. Therefore, turmeric capsules can be an effective natural remedy for managing jaundice.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric capsules can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for jaundice as it helps to improve liver function and bile flow. This property is due to the active constituent curcumin.

Antioxidant: Turmeric capsules have antioxidant properties that can help protect liver cells from damage, which is important for jaundice as it helps to prevent further liver damage. The active constituent curcumin is responsible for this property.

Cholagogue: Turmeric capsules can stimulate the production of bile and its flow from the liver, which is important for jaundice as it helps to eliminate bilirubin from the body. The active constituent curcumin contributes to this property.

Hepatoprotective: Turmeric capsules can protect the liver from toxic damage, which is important for jaundice as it helps to prevent further liver damage. The active constituent curcumin is responsible for this property.

Antibacterial: Turmeric capsules have antibacterial properties that can help prevent infections in the liver or bile ducts, which is important for jaundice as it helps to prevent complications. The active constituents curcumin and turmerone contribute to this property.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's rhizomes are commonly used to make medicinal tea for jaundice due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help support liver function.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat jaundice, as they contain curcumin, which helps stimulate bile production and flow.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant are rich in curcumin and other beneficial compounds, making them a popular choice for medicinal tea to support liver health and treat jaundice symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather and measure ingredients: 1 cup of turmeric powder, 1/2 cup of fenugreek seeds, 1/4 cup of fennel seeds, and 1/4 cup of dried licorice root.
Grind the fenugreek seeds, fennel seeds, and dried licorice root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Mix the ground seeds and licorice root with the turmeric powder in a large bowl.
Fill size 00 gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry place, and take 2-3 capsules daily with meals for best results. Preparation time is approximately 1 hour, depending on the efficiency of the grinder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common barberry capsule helps with jaundice because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that has a proven antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effect. Jaundice is often caused by an infection or inflammation of the liver, so the anti-inflammatory properties of barberry can help reduce inflammation in the liver and promote healing. Additionally, barberry stimulates the production of bile, which is necessary for the digestion of fats and the elimination of waste products from the liver. This increased bile production can help to reduce the levels of bilirubin in the blood, which is a key characteristic of jaundice. Overall, the combination of anti-inflammatory and bile-stimulating effects of barberry make it a valuable herbal remedy for jaundice.
remedy_properties: Cholagogue: Barberry stimulates the flow of bile from the liver, which is important for jaundice as it helps eliminate bilirubin, a pigment that accumulates in the blood in jaundice, leading to its yellowish discoloration. The active constituents responsible for this property are the isoquinoline alkaloids, particularly berberine.

Hepatoprotective: Barberry has a protective effect on the liver, which is crucial in jaundice as it helps reduce liver damage and inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include berberine, oxycanthine, and palmatine.

Antioxidant: Barberry contains potent antioxidants that help combat oxidative stress and free radical damage in the liver, making it an essential property for managing jaundice. The primary active constituents with antioxidant properties are berberine and anthocyanins.

Anti-inflammatory: Barberry has anti-inflammatory properties, which are vital for jaundice as they help reduce inflammation in the liver and bile ducts, promoting better bile flow and overall liver function. The main active constituents responsible for this property are berberine, oxycanthine, and palmatine.

Antimicrobial: Barberry has antimicrobial properties, which are significant for jaundice as they help prevent and treat infections in the liver and bile ducts, reducing the risk of complications. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are berberine and oxycanthine.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and alkaloids, leaves are used to make medicinal tea for jaundice due to their liver-protecting and detoxifying properties.
Roots: Containing berberine, the roots are used for their anti-inflammatory and choleretic effects, which help in treating jaundice.
Bark: The bark is used for its hepatoprotective and antioxidant properties, making it suitable for jaundice treatment.
Seeds: Seeds are rich in essential oils and have been used in medicinal teas for jaundice due to their choleretic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried common barberry root bark and grind it into a fine powder.
Mix the powder with 0.5 teaspoons of a binding agent, such as gelatin or starch.
Using a capsule filling machine or by hand, fill size "00" empty capsules with the barberry-binding agent mixture.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 24 hours in a cool, dark place.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container, away from direct sunlight, for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsule helps with jaundice because it contains silymarin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have protective effects on the liver. Jaundice is a condition characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes due to the buildup of bilirubin in the body, often caused by liver problems. Silymarin in milk thistle capsules helps to promote the regeneration of liver cells, reduce inflammation, and improve the flow of bile, which can help to reduce the levels of bilirubin in the body. Additionally, milk thistle has antioxidant properties that can help to protect the liver from further damage, making it a useful supplement for those suffering from jaundice.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Milk thistle capsules can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for jaundice as it helps to alleviate damage to liver cells and promote healing. This property is due to the active constituent, silymarin.

Antioxidant: Milk thistle has antioxidant properties that protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for jaundice because it can help to prevent further liver damage. Silymarin is also responsible for this medicinal property.

Hepatoprotective: Milk thistle has a liver-protecting effect, which is important for jaundice as it can help to prevent and treat liver damage. The active constituent responsible for this property is silymarin.

Regenerative: Milk thistle can stimulate the growth of new liver cells, which is important for jaundice as it can help to replace damaged liver cells. This property is due to the active constituent, silymarin.

Cholagogue: Milk thistle can promote the flow of bile from the liver, which is important for jaundice as it can help to reduce the buildup of bilirubin in the body. The active constituents responsible for this property are silymarin and silybin.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the milk thistle plant contain silymarin, a flavonoid that is known for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating jaundice.
Leaves: The leaves of the milk thistle plant are rich in nutrients and have been used in traditional medicine to support liver function and treat jaundice.
Flowers: The flowers of the milk thistle plant contain bioflavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in the treatment of jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried milk thistle seeds and a capsule-making kit, ensuring the seeds are organically sourced and the kit includes empty vegetarian capsules and a manual grinder.
Grind 200-250 milligrams of milk thistle seeds into a fine powder using the manual grinder, then evenly fill the vegetarian capsules, following the kit's instructions for proper assembly.
Store the filled capsules in a tightly sealed glass container, away from direct sunlight and heat, for up to six months.
Consult a healthcare professional for appropriate dosage and administration, as it may vary depending on the individual's age, health status, and severity of jaundice.
Take the milk thistle capsules as directed by the healthcare professional, typically 1-2 capsules daily, for a minimum of three months to see improvements in jaundice symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Katuki capsule helps with jaundice because it contains the extract of Picrorhiza kurroa, a herb that has been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for centuries. This herb has been found to have hepatoprotective properties, which means it can protect the liver from damage. Katuki capsule stimulates the flow of bile, which is necessary for the digestion and absorption of fats and fat-soluble vitamins. It also helps to reduce inflammation and promote the regeneration of liver cells. Jaundice is a condition characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes due to the buildup of bilirubin in the body. Katuki capsule helps to reduce bilirubin levels by promoting the flow of bile and supporting liver function.
remedy_properties: Cholagogue: Katuki capsules stimulate the flow of bile from the liver, which is important for jaundice as it helps remove bilirubin from the body. The active constituents responsible for this property are the iridoid glycosides, such as swertiamarin and sweroside.

Hepatoprotective: Katuki capsules protect the liver from damage, which is crucial in jaundice as it helps prevent further liver dysfunction. The hepatoprotective effects are attributed to the presence of bitter principles like gentiopicroside and swertiamarin.

Anti-inflammatory: Katuki capsules reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for jaundice as it alleviates liver congestion and swelling. The anti-inflammatory properties are due to the iridoid glycosides and flavonoids present in the herb.

Antioxidant: Katuki capsules have antioxidant properties that help protect liver cells from oxidative stress, which is significant for jaundice as it reduces free radical damage. The antioxidant activity is attributed to the presence of flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids.

Diuretic: Katuki capsules promote urination, which helps eliminate waste products and toxins from the body. This property is important for jaundice as it aids in the removal of bilirubin and other harmful substances. The diuretic effects are due to the presence of various compounds, including tannins and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and known to have a positive effect on liver function, making them effective in treating jaundice.
Roots: Contain powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to support liver health and reduce jaundice symptoms.
Flowers: High in flavonoids, known to improve liver function, reduce oxidative stress, and help manage jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 500mg of katuki root powder and combine it with 100mg of licorice root powder and 100mg of milk thistle seed powder.
Mix the powders thoroughly to ensure even distribution of each ingredient.
Fill size "0" gelatin capsules with the powder mixture, using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.
Take one capsule three times daily, preferably after meals, for best results in managing jaundice symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Andrographis capsule helps with jaundice because it contains Andrographis paniculata, a herb that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential hepatoprotective properties. The herb contains diterpene lactones, including andrographolide, which may help to protect the liver and promote bile secretion. This can be beneficial for individuals with jaundice, a condition characterized by the yellowing of the skin and eyes due to the buildup of bilirubin in the body. By supporting liver function and bile secretion, andrographis capsules may help to reduce bilirubin levels and alleviate the symptoms of jaundice. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before taking any supplements for jaundice or any other medical condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Andrographis capsules can help reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for jaundice as it can alleviate the symptoms caused by the obstruction of bile ducts, and the active constituents responsible for this property are andrographolide and deoxyandrographolide.

Hepatoprotective: These capsules can protect the liver from damage, which is crucial for jaundice as it can help prevent further liver dysfunction, and the key active constituents providing this benefit are andrographolide, neoandrographolide, and 14-deoxyandrographolide.

Antioxidant: Andrographis capsules have antioxidant properties that can help combat oxidative stress in the liver, which is significant for jaundice as it can minimize the risk of liver damage, and the active constituents responsible for this property are andrographolide, 14-deoxyandrographolide, and skypherol.

Choleretic: These capsules can increase bile secretion, which is important for jaundice as it can help eliminate the excess bilirubin accumulated in the body, and the active constituents responsible for this property are andrographolide and its derivatives.

Immunomodulatory: Andrographis capsules can regulate the immune system, which is beneficial for jaundice as it can help reduce inflammation and prevent complications, and the active constituents responsible for this property are andrographolide, deoxyandrographolide, and 14-deoxyandrographolide.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the andrographis capsule plant are commonly used to make medicinal tea for jaundice due to their bitter taste and high concentration of andrographolides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and liver-protecting properties.

Leaves: The leaves of the andrographis capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for jaundice because they contain andrographolides and other compounds that are thought to support liver function and reduce inflammation.

Flowers: The flowers of the andrographis capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for jaundice because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that are believed to have antioxidant and liver-protecting properties.

Seeds: The seeds of the andrographis capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for jaundice because they contain andrographolides and other compounds that are thought to have anti-inflammatory and liver-protecting properties.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried Andrographis paniculata herb, equivalent to 450mg per capsule, and size 00 empty vegetarian capsules.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure 450mg of the Andrographis powder and carefully fill each empty capsule, using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the filled capsules in a tightly sealed container, in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight.
Consult a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen, especially if you have a medical condition or are taking prescription medications.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stonebreaker capsule helps with jaundice because it contains ingredients that promote bile production and flow, which aids in the breakdown and elimination of bilirubin, a compound that accumulates in the blood during jaundice. The herbal ingredients in the capsule also have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce liver inflammation and damage, promoting liver health and function. Furthermore, stonebreaker capsule may help protect the liver from toxins and support the regeneration of liver cells, which can be beneficial in managing jaundice.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Stonebreaker capsules help increase the production of urine, which is important for jaundice as it helps eliminate bilirubin from the body. This property is attributed to the presence of potent flavonoids and sterols in the plant.

Cholagogue property: Stonebreaker capsules promote the flow of bile from the liver, which is crucial for jaundice as it helps remove bilirubin from the blood. The active constituents responsible for this property include phyllanthin and hypophyllanthin.

Hepatoprotective property: Stonebreaker capsules protect the liver from damage, which is important for jaundice as it helps prevent further liver dysfunction. This property is attributed to the presence of tannins, lignans, and alkaloids in the plant.

Antioxidant property: Stonebreaker capsules neutralize free radicals, which is important for jaundice as it helps reduce oxidative stress in the liver. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids in the plant.

Anti-inflammatory property: Stonebreaker capsules reduce inflammation, which is important for jaundice as it helps alleviate symptoms such as pain and swelling in the liver. This property is attributed to the presence of terpenoids, alkaloids, and lignans in the plant.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, the leaves are used to make a bitter tea that promotes bile production and aids in liver function.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, the flowers help soothe liver inflammation and reduce jaundice symptoms.
Roots: High in alkaloids, the roots are used to create a potent tea that stimulates bile secretion and supports liver health.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, the seeds are used to make a fragrant tea that aids in digestion and alleviates jaundice-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dry stonebreaker herb (Phyllanthus niruri) and measure out 2 teaspoons.
Grind the herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground herb with 1 teaspoon of a binding agent, such as gelatin or plant-based capsule material.
Fill size 00 empty capsules with the herb and binding agent mixture using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed stonebreaker capsules in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Punarnava capsule helps with jaundice because it contains Punarnava, a potent herb known for its hepatoprotective properties. This herb aids in the elimination of toxins from the body, supports liver function, and reduces bilirubin levels. Its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties help in reducing inflammation and managing symptoms associated with jaundice. Regular consumption of Punarnava capsules can help alleviate jaundice-related discomfort, promote liver health, and support overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Punarnava capsules have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the liver, a common symptom in jaundice, mainly due to the presence of alkaloids, steroids, and flavonoids.

Hepatoprotective: Punarnava capsules protect the liver from damage caused by toxins and infections, which is crucial in jaundice treatment, due to the hepatoprotective effects of punarnavine and lignans.

Diuretic: Punarnava capsules promote diuresis, helping the body eliminate toxins and excess bilirubin, a key factor in jaundice, through the active constituents, such as punarnavoside and steroidal glycosides.

Antioxidant: Punarnava capsules contain powerful antioxidants that help combat oxidative stress and free radicals in the liver, which is essential in jaundice management, due to the antioxidant properties of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and tannins.

Cholagogue: Punarnava capsules stimulate the flow of bile from the liver and gallbladder, aiding in the digestion process and elimination of bilirubin, which is beneficial in jaundice treatment, thanks to the cholagogue effects of the herb's alkaloids and saponins.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the punarnava capsule plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for jaundice due to their anti-inflammatory and detoxifying properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the punarnava capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for jaundice because they contain compounds that help to protect the liver and promote bile production.
Flowers: The flowers of the punarnava capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for jaundice as they have hepatoprotective properties, which means they can help to protect the liver from damage.
Seeds: The seeds of the punarnava capsule plant are used in medicinal tea for jaundice as they contain high levels of antioxidants, which can help to reduce oxidative stress in the liver.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of Punarnava leaves and 20 grams of coriander seeds, then clean and dry them for 24 hours.
Grind the dried Punarnava leaves and coriander seeds into a fine powder, mixing them thoroughly for even consistency.
Add 10 grams of turmeric powder and 5 grams of black pepper powder to the mixture, blending well.
Fill size 00 vegetarian capsules with the herbal blend, using approximately 500mg per capsule.
Seal the capsules and store in a cool, dry place, taking two capsules twice daily after meals for jaundice treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsule helps with jaundice because it contains active compounds that have been shown to support liver function. The main component, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may protect the liver from damage and promote the regeneration of liver cells. Licorice also stimulates the production of bile, which is essential for the digestion of fats and the elimination of toxins from the body. By improving liver function and bile flow, licorice capsules can help reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, alleviating the symptoms of jaundice. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before taking licorice capsules, as they can interact with certain medications and may not be suitable for people with certain medical conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice capsules can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for jaundice as it helps to alleviate the damage done to liver cells, and this property is primarily due to the presence of glycyrrhizin.

Hepatoprotective: Licorice has the ability to protect liver cells from damage, which is crucial for jaundice as it helps to prevent further liver dysfunction, and this is attributed to the active constituents such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Licorice contains antioxidants that can help to neutralize free radicals, which is significant for jaundice as it helps to reduce oxidative stress in the liver, and this is mainly due to the presence of flavonoids.

Choleretic: Licorice can stimulate the production of bile, which is important for jaundice as it helps to promote the elimination of bilirubin, and this property is primarily due to the presence of glycyrrhetinic acid.

Immune-boosting: Licorice can enhance the immune system, which is significant for jaundice as it helps to combat any underlying infections that may be contributing to the condition, and this is attributed to the active constituents such as glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice capsule plant contain glycyrrhizin, which helps to protect the liver and promote bile secretion, making it effective in treating jaundice.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice capsule plant have hepatoprotective properties, which help to protect the liver and promote its function, aiding in the treatment of jaundice.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice capsule plant contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress in jaundice.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice capsule plant contain coumarins, which have been shown to improve liver function and reduce bilirubin levels, making them useful in the treatment of jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1000mg of dried licorice root and 250mg of dried milk thistle, both finely ground.
Combine the ground licorice root and milk thistle in a bowl, mixing thoroughly.
Load size "0" empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Fill a clean, airtight container with the prepared capsules, storing in a cool, dry place.
Take one capsule three times daily, 30 minutes before meals, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ajwain capsule helps with jaundice because it contains thymol, a compound that stimulates the production of bile and gastric juices, which aids in digestion and liver function. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of ajwain also help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, promoting its healing and rejuvenation. Furthermore, ajwain has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, which means it can protect the liver from damage caused by toxins and infections. As a result, ajwain capsules can help alleviate the symptoms of jaundice, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, loss of appetite, and abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-oxidant properties: Ajwain contains tannins and flavonoids which have antioxidant properties that help protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals, which is important in managing jaundice.

Hepatoprotective properties: Ajwain has hepatoprotective properties due to the presence of thymol, which helps in the regeneration of liver cells and protects them from further damage, making it beneficial in jaundice treatment.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ajwain contains compounds like thymol and limonene that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the liver and bile ducts, alleviating jaundice symptoms.

Carminative properties: Ajwain has carminative properties due to the presence of thymol and other essential oils, which help in stimulating the digestive system, reducing gastric acidity, and improving overall digestion, all of which are crucial in managing jaundice.

Diuretic properties: Ajwain has diuretic properties due to its potassium content and essential oils, which help in increasing urine output, thereby aiding in the elimination of toxins from the body and supporting liver function during jaundice.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the ajwain capsule plant contain thymol, which is known to stimulate bile production and help in the treatment of jaundice.
Leaves: The leaves of the ajwain capsule plant have hepatoprotective properties, making them useful for treating liver disorders such as jaundice.
Flowers: The flowers of the ajwain capsule plant contain antioxidants that help in the detoxification of the liver and aid in the treatment of jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of ajwain seeds and 1 teaspoon of dried neem leaves, then grind them into a fine powder.
Mix the powdered herbs with 1 teaspoon of crushed turmeric root and 1 teaspoon of powdered licorice root.
Add just enough water to create a thick paste, then shape the mixture into small, equal-sized capsules.
Allow the capsules to dry for 24 hours at room temperature, away from direct sunlight.
Store the dried ajwain capsules in an airtight container for up to 3 months, taking 2 capsules daily after meals for jaundice relief.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaundice with herbal capsules is by using a combination of milk thistle, turmeric, and dandelion. These herbs are known to support liver function, promote bile production, and aid in detoxification. Milk thistle contains silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that protects liver cells from damage. Turmeric has curcumin, which reduces inflammation and helps in regeneration of liver tissues. Dandelion stimulates bile production and flow, improving overall liver function. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaundice that are treated with herbal capsules include liver disorders such as hepatitis, fatty liver disease, and cirrhosis. These conditions, like jaundice, are often characterized by a yellowing of the skin and eyes due to an accumulation of bilirubin. Herbal remedies, in the form of capsules, can help support liver function, reduce inflammation, and promote the flow of bile. Some commonly used herbs include milk thistle, dandelion root, turmeric, and artichoke. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.
